commands.review_summary:
The PR generates `public/llms.txt` from ClawHub registry and documentation metadata, wires generation into dev/build/static checks, and centralizes shared registry URLs used by navigation and the generator.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a new build/docs metadata capability rather than a bug report. The PR body provides terminal proof for generation and local serving of `/llms.txt`.
commands.review_followup: - No narrow automated repair is evident after the latest force-push; the remaining action is normal maintainer review and Vercel authorization handling.

target.title: build: generate llms.txt from canonical metadata
target.body:
## Summary

This PR generates `public/llms.txt` from canonical ClawHub metadata instead of maintaining the deployed file by hand.

- Discovers publishable `docs/*.md` pages from their frontmatter, with an explicit `llms: false` opt-out.
- Centralizes canonical registry paths and external URLs so navigation and `llms.txt` share the same source of truth.
- Generates the asset before local development and production builds.
- Validates the rendered output in the static CI gate.
- Writes atomically and skips unchanged output.

The generated file is ignored by Git and is emitted into the production bundle as `.output/public/llms.txt`.

## Validation

- `bunx vitest run scripts/generate-llms-txt.test.ts src/__tests__/ui-design-contract.test.ts src/__tests__/header.test.tsx src/components/Footer.test.tsx`
- `bun run ci:static`
- `bun run ci:unit`
- `bun run ci:types-build`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `$autoreview` branch review: no accepted or actionable findings

The focused tests cover automatic documentation discovery, explicit exclusion, invalid metadata, deterministic canonical output, ASCII/newline constraints, and no-op atomic rewrites.

Generated-file proof:

```text
$ bunx vitest run scripts/generate-llms-txt.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       4 passed (4)

$ bun run llms:generate
$ bun run llms:check

$ file public/llms.txt
public/llms.txt: ASCII text

$ wc -c -l public/llms.txt
57 4915 public/llms.txt

$ cmp -s public/llms.txt .output/public/llms.txt
exit 0
```

Local served-file proof:

```text
GET http://127.0.0.1:4183/llms.txt
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: text/plain
Content-Length: 4915
```

The served file matched the generated source byte-for-byte. The production build output also matched `public/llms.txt`.

## Generated `llms.txt` after this routine

```markdown
# ClawHub

> ClawHub is the public registry for OpenClaw skills and plugins.

Use OpenClaw to search, install, and update registry content. Use the ClawHub CLI for registry authentication, publishing, and listing management. Follow the canonical documentation below for current commands and workflows.

This generated file is a machine-readable map, not an access-control policy or a substitute for reviewing an artifact before installation.

## Primary

- [ClawHub](https://clawhub.ai): Browse the public registry for skills, plugins, and publishers.
- [ClawHub documentation](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/): Canonical user, publisher, API, trust, and operations documentation.
- [OpenClaw docs llms.txt](https://docs.openclaw.ai/llms.txt): Machine-readable map for the full OpenClaw documentation set.
- [OpenAPI v1](https://clawhub.ai/api/v1/openapi.json): Machine-readable schema for the current ClawHub HTTP API.

## Public Registry Surfaces

- [Skills](https://clawhub.ai/skills): Browse and search skill bundles centered on SKILL.md.
- [Plugins and packages](https://clawhub.ai/plugins): Browse and search OpenClaw plugin package records.
- [Publishers](https://clawhub.ai/publishers): Browse public user and organization publishers.
- [Search](https://clawhub.ai/search): Search the public ClawHub catalog.
- [Security audits](https://clawhub.ai/audits): Browse public artifact security audit results.
- [Publish a skill](https://clawhub.ai/skills/publish): Publish a new skill or update an existing skill.
- [Publish a plugin](https://clawhub.ai/plugins/publish): Publish an OpenClaw plugin package.

## Documentation

- [ClawHub](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/): Public ClawHub overview for discovery, install, publish, security, and the clawhub CLI.
- [API v1](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/api): Public REST API (v1) overview and conventions.
- [Acceptable Usage](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/acceptable-usage): Marketplace policy: what ClawHub allows and what it will not host.
- [Auth](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/auth): ClawHub sign-in, API tokens, CLI login, token storage, and revocation.
- [CLI](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/cli): CLI reference: commands, flags, config, and lockfile behavior.
- [Content Rights Requests](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/content-rights): How to submit a copyright or other content rights concern about material published on ClawHub.
- [HTTP API](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/http-api): HTTP API reference (public + CLI endpoints + auth).
- [How ClawHub Works](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/how-it-works): How ClawHub listings, versions, installs, publishing, scans, and updates work.
- [Moderation and Account Safety](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/moderation): How ClawHub reports, moderation holds, hidden listings, bans, and account standing work.
- [Plugin validation fixes](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/plugin-validation-fixes): Fix ClawHub plugin package validation findings before publishing
- [Publishing](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/publishing): How ClawHub publishing works for skills, plugins, owners, scopes, releases, and review.
- [Quickstart](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/quickstart): Start using ClawHub: find, install, update, and publish skills or plugins.
- [Security](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/security): How to report ClawHub security issues and when vulnerabilities are publicly disclosed.
- [Security Audits](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/security-audits): How to understand ClawHub security audit results before installing a skill or plugin.
- [Skill format](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/skill-format): Skill folder format, required files, allowed file types, limits.
- [Telemetry](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/telemetry): Install telemetry collected by the ClawHub CLI and how to opt out.
- [Troubleshooting](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/troubleshooting): Troubleshooting ClawHub sign-in, install, publish, update, and API issues.

## Source

- [Source repository](https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub): TanStack Start app, Convex backend, CLI packages, public docs, and specifications.
- [Public docs source](https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub/tree/main/docs): Source Markdown mirrored into the OpenClaw documentation site.
- [Schema package](https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub/tree/main/packages/schema): Shared API routes, package schemas, and compatibility contracts.
- [ClawHub CLI](https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub/tree/main/packages/clawhub): CLI implementation for authentication, discovery, publishing, and listing management.
- [Convex backend](https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub/tree/main/convex): Registry data, HTTP handlers, publishing, scans, moderation, and search.

## Optional

- [robots.txt](https://clawhub.ai/robots.txt): Crawler policy for the ClawHub site.
- [Web app manifest](https://clawhub.ai/manifest.json): Installable web app metadata.
```

The Vercel preview remains blocked by team authorization, independently of this diff.
